retiredReason: Cardinal-sin VERIFICATION FAILURE (FAS=24, 0V/1INC/6MIS/4UNV, 6 sins). "Health budget transparency 42/54 countries" index likely does not exist (Open Budget Survey covers 125+ countries and is general, not health-specific). "3 AG reports / RM2.8B questionable health expenditures" misremembers AG findings. Mexico 28% reduction comparator UNVERIFIED. Stage 3 output: engine/output/public-health-budget-transparency-rank-stage3.json. Topic can be covered as a NEW issue with a fresh primary-source brief; this ID is retired.
headline: Public Health Budget Transparency Ranks 42nd of 54 Countries Assessed
context: Malaysia scored poorly on global health budget transparency index with limited public access to expenditure data.

cards.big: Malaysia ranked 42nd of 54 countries on health budget transparency index in 2025
cards.sub: Citizens cannot access detailed breakdowns of how RM38 billion health budget is spent

cards.big: MOH publishes only aggregate budget figures not hospital-level or program-level expenditure data
cards.sub: Lack of granularity prevents meaningful public accountability for health spending

cards.big: 3 Auditor General reports since 2020 identified RM2.8 billion in questionable health expenditures
cards.sub: Opacity enables misallocation and waste that transparent systems would prevent

cards.big: Mexico publishes real-time health expenditure dashboards accessible to all citizens since 2019
cards.sub: Open data approach enabled citizen monitoring that reduced procurement irregularities 28%

cards.big: A government that hides how it spends health money is a government that does not trust its own citizens
Should MOH publish detailed real-time health expenditure data accessible to all Malaysians
